description: The mathematics of continuous change: limits, derivatives, integrals, and their generalizations to multiple variables and vector fields.

concepts.name: Limit of a Function
concepts.description: lim_{x→a} f(x) = L means f(x) can be made arbitrarily close to L by taking x sufficiently close (but not equal) to a; formalized by the ε-δ definition.

concepts.name: Limit Laws
concepts.description: The sum, difference, product, quotient, and power rules for limits: lim(f±g) = limf ± limg, lim(fg) = (limf)(limg), etc.

concepts.name: One-Sided Limits
concepts.description: The right-hand limit lim_{x→a⁺} f(x) and left-hand limit lim_{x→a⁻} f(x); the two-sided limit exists iff both one-sided limits exist and are equal.

concepts.name: Limits at Infinity
concepts.description: lim_{x→∞} f(x) = L means f(x) → L as x grows without bound; determines horizontal asymptotes of rational and other functions.

concepts.name: Squeeze Theorem
concepts.description: If g(x) ≤ f(x) ≤ h(x) near a and lim g = lim h = L, then lim f = L; used to compute limits like lim(sinx/x) = 1.

concepts.name: Continuity
concepts.description: f is continuous at a if lim_{x→a} f(x) = f(a); on an interval if continuous at every point; the calculus definition to be sharpened in real analysis.

concepts.name: Types of Discontinuity
concepts.description: Removable (hole), jump (left/right limits exist but differ), and infinite (vertical asymptote) discontinuities; classified by one-sided limits.

concepts.name: Intermediate Value Theorem
concepts.description: If f is continuous on [a, b] and N is between f(a) and f(b), then there exists c ∈ (a, b) with f(c) = N; guarantees roots and intermediate values.

concepts.name: Slope of Tangent and Rate of Change
concepts.description: The derivative is motivated by the limit of the average rate of change (difference quotient) as the interval shrinks to zero, giving the instantaneous rate.

concepts.name: Derivative (Definition)
concepts.description: f'(x) = lim_{h→0} [f(x+h) − f(x)] / h; the instantaneous rate of change of f at x; the slope of the tangent line to the graph at x.

concepts.name: Differentiability
concepts.description: f is differentiable at a if f'(a) exists; differentiability implies continuity but not vice versa; non-differentiable at corners, cusps, and vertical tangents.

concepts.name: Basic Differentiation Rules
concepts.description: Power rule: d/dx(xⁿ) = nxⁿ⁻¹; constant multiple rule; sum/difference rule; enable differentiation of all polynomials.

concepts.name: Product Rule
concepts.description: (fg)' = f'g + fg'; enables differentiation of products of functions.

concepts.name: Quotient Rule
concepts.description: (f/g)' = (f'g − fg')/g²; enables differentiation of quotients; derivable from product rule and chain rule.

concepts.name: Chain Rule
concepts.description: The derivative of a composite function: (f∘g)'(x) = f'(g(x)) · g'(x); one of the most frequently used differentiation rules.

concepts.name: Derivative of Exponential Functions
concepts.description: d/dx(eˣ) = eˣ (eˣ is its own derivative); d/dx(aˣ) = aˣ ln a; derived via the chain rule and the natural log definition.

concepts.name: Derivative of Logarithmic Functions
concepts.description: d/dx(ln x) = 1/x; d/dx(logₐ x) = 1/(x ln a); logarithmic differentiation simplifies products and quotients raised to powers.

concepts.name: Logarithmic Differentiation
concepts.description: Taking ln of both sides before differentiating; required for variable-base-and-exponent forms like y = xˢⁱⁿˣ.

concepts.name: Derivatives of Trigonometric Functions
concepts.description: d/dx(sin x) = cos x; d/dx(cos x) = −sin x; d/dx(tan x) = sec²x; and their reciprocals; all derivable from the definition and squeeze theorem.

concepts.name: Derivatives of Inverse Trig Functions
concepts.description: d/dx(arcsin x) = 1/√(1−x²); d/dx(arctan x) = 1/(1+x²); derived via implicit differentiation of the inverse relation.

concepts.name: Derivatives of Hyperbolic Functions
concepts.description: d/dx(sinh x) = cosh x; d/dx(cosh x) = sinh x; analogous to trig but without sign changes; derivable directly from exponential definitions.

concepts.name: Implicit Differentiation
concepts.description: Differentiating both sides of an equation in x and y with respect to x, treating y as a function of x via the chain rule; gives dy/dx without solving for y.

concepts.name: Higher-Order Derivatives
concepts.description: The second derivative f'' = (f')' measures the rate of change of the slope; higher derivatives arise in Taylor series, ODEs, and curvature.

concepts.name: Related Rates
concepts.description: Using implicit differentiation with respect to time to relate the rates of change of multiple related quantities; e.g., expanding balloon radius and volume.

concepts.name: L'Hôpital's Rule
concepts.description: If lim f/g is indeterminate (0/0 or ∞/∞), then lim f/g = lim f'/g' under appropriate conditions; handles indeterminate forms like 0·∞, 1^∞, etc.

concepts.name: Mean Value Theorem
concepts.description: If f is continuous on [a,b] and differentiable on (a,b), there exists c ∈ (a,b) with f'(c) = (f(b)−f(a))/(b−a); the average rate equals the instantaneous rate somewhere.

concepts.name: Rolle's Theorem
concepts.description: Special case of MVT: if f is continuous on [a,b], differentiable on (a,b), and f(a) = f(b), then there exists c ∈ (a,b) with f'(c) = 0.

concepts.name: Increasing and Decreasing Functions
concepts.description: If f' > 0 on an interval, f is increasing; if f' < 0, f is decreasing; follows from the MVT.

concepts.name: Critical Points
concepts.description: Points where f'(c) = 0 or f'(c) is undefined; candidates for local maxima, minima, and inflection points.

concepts.name: Local Extrema
concepts.description: Local max/min at critical points c; first derivative test: sign change of f'; second derivative test: f''(c) > 0 → local min, f''(c) < 0 → local max.

concepts.name: Concavity and Inflection Points
concepts.description: f is concave up where f'' > 0 (cup shape) and concave down where f'' < 0 (cap shape); inflection points where concavity changes.

concepts.name: Optimization (Calculus)
concepts.description: Finding global maxima and minima of a function on a closed interval or over all reals using critical points, endpoints, and boundary analysis.

concepts.name: Curve Sketching
concepts.description: Systematic method to sketch y = f(x) using domain, intercepts, symmetry, asymptotes, increasing/decreasing intervals, and concavity.

concepts.name: Linearization and Differentials
concepts.description: Approximating f(x) near a by its tangent line L(x) = f(a) + f'(a)(x−a); the differential df = f'(x)dx is the linearization of the increment.

concepts.name: Antiderivatives
concepts.description: F is an antiderivative of f if F' = f; uniquely determined up to a constant; written ∫f(x)dx = F(x) + C.

concepts.name: Riemann Sums
concepts.description: Approximations of the area under a curve by summing areas of rectangles with widths Δx and heights f(xᵢ*); the definite integral is their limit as Δx → 0.

concepts.name: Definite Integral
concepts.description: The limit of Riemann sums lim_{n→∞} Σf(xᵢ*)Δx = ∫ₐᵇ f(x)dx; measures signed area between f and the x-axis from a to b.

concepts.name: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus Part 1
concepts.description: If F(x) = ∫ₐˣ f(t)dt and f is continuous, then F'(x) = f(x); differentiation and integration are inverse operations.

concepts.name: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus Part 2
concepts.description: If F is any antiderivative of f, then ∫ₐᵇ f(x)dx = F(b) − F(a); enables exact evaluation of definite integrals.

concepts.name: Integration by Substitution
concepts.description: Reversing the chain rule: ∫f(g(x))g'(x)dx = ∫f(u)du where u = g(x); the most fundamental integration technique.

concepts.name: Integration by Parts
concepts.description: ∫u dv = uv − ∫v du; reverses the product rule; used for products of trig, exponential, log, and polynomial functions.

concepts.name: Trigonometric Integrals
concepts.description: Integration of products of trig functions using Pythagorean identities, double-angle formulas, and reduction formulas; includes ∫sinⁿx dx, ∫tanⁿx sec^m x dx.

concepts.name: Trigonometric Substitution
concepts.description: Substituting x = a sin θ, a tan θ, or a sec θ to eliminate square roots of the forms a²−x², a²+x², x²−a² in integrands.

concepts.name: Partial Fraction Decomposition
concepts.description: Decomposing a rational function p(x)/q(x) (deg p < deg q) into a sum of simpler fractions; enables integration of all rational functions.

concepts.name: Reduction Formulas
concepts.description: Formulas expressing ∫f^n in terms of ∫f^(n-2) or ∫f^(n-1); used for ∫sinⁿx dx, ∫cosⁿx dx, ∫xⁿeˣdx.

concepts.name: Improper Integrals
concepts.description: Definite integrals with infinite limits or integrands with singularities; defined as limits of proper integrals; convergent or divergent.

concepts.name: Area by Integration
concepts.description: Area under y = f(x) from a to b is ∫ₐᵇ f(x)dx; area between curves is ∫ₐᵇ [f(x)−g(x)]dx where f ≥ g.

concepts.name: Volumes of Revolution
concepts.description: Volume of solid formed by rotating a region: disk method V = π∫f²dx; washer method V = π∫(f²−g²)dx; shell method V = 2π∫xf(x)dx.

concepts.name: Arc Length
concepts.description: The length of a curve y = f(x) from a to b: L = ∫ₐᵇ √(1 + [f'(x)]²) dx; generalizes to parametric and polar curves.

concepts.name: Surface Area of Revolution
concepts.description: The surface area generated by rotating y = f(x) about the x-axis: S = 2π∫ₐᵇ f(x)√(1+[f'(x)]²) dx.

concepts.name: Power Series
concepts.description: A series Σcₙ(x−a)ⁿ defining a function on an interval |x−a| < R where R is the radius of convergence; the natural setting for Taylor series.

concepts.name: Radius of Convergence
concepts.description: The radius R such that the power series converges absolutely for |x−a| < R and diverges for |x−a| > R; found via the ratio or root test.

concepts.name: Taylor Series
concepts.description: Representing a smooth function as a power series about a point a: f(x) = Σf^(n)(a)(x−a)ⁿ/n!; valid on the radius of convergence.

concepts.name: Maclaurin Series
concepts.description: Taylor series centered at a = 0; key examples: eˣ = Σxⁿ/n!, sin x = Σ(−1)ⁿx^(2n+1)/(2n+1)!, cos x = Σ(−1)ⁿx^(2n)/(2n)!, 1/(1−x) = Σxⁿ.

concepts.name: Taylor Remainder and Error Bound
concepts.description: The error in approximating f by its nth Taylor polynomial: |Rₙ(x)| ≤ M|x−a|^(n+1)/(n+1)! where M bounds |f^(n+1)|; enables precision control.

concepts.name: Parametric Equations and Curves
concepts.description: Describing a curve by parametric equations x = f(t), y = g(t); enables representation of curves that are not functions, like circles and epicycloids.

concepts.name: Calculus of Parametric Curves
concepts.description: Differentiation: dy/dx = (dy/dt)/(dx/dt); arc length: L = ∫√((dx/dt)² + (dy/dt)²) dt; area under parametric curve.

concepts.name: Introduction to Multivariable Calculus
concepts.description: Extension of calculus to functions of two or more variables; the domain becomes a subset of ℝⁿ and the range ℝᵐ.

concepts.name: Partial Derivatives
concepts.description: The partial derivative ∂f/∂x is the derivative of f with respect to x holding all other variables constant; measures rate of change in one coordinate direction.

concepts.name: Gradient
concepts.description: ∇f = (∂f/∂x, ∂f/∂y, …); the vector of partial derivatives pointing in the direction of steepest ascent; its magnitude is the maximum rate of change.

concepts.name: Directional Derivative
aliases: rate of change in direction u
concepts.description: The rate of change of f at a point in the direction of unit vector u: Dᵤf = ∇f · u; maximized when u is parallel to ∇f.

concepts.name: Multivariable Chain Rule
concepts.description: Generalizes the chain rule to functions of multiple variables via a tree of partial derivatives; fundamental for implicit differentiation in multiple variables.

concepts.name: Multivariable Extrema
concepts.description: Critical points where ∇f = 0; classified via the Hessian matrix: D > 0 and fₓₓ > 0 → local min, D > 0 and fₓₓ < 0 → local max, D < 0 → saddle point.

concepts.name: Multiple Integrals
concepts.description: Extensions of the definite integral to functions of two or three variables; computed via iterated integrals using Fubini's theorem.

concepts.name: Double Integrals
concepts.description: ∬_D f(x,y)dA computes volume under a surface over region D; evaluated by Fubini's theorem as an iterated integral; also in polar coordinates.

concepts.name: Triple Integrals
concepts.description: ∭_E f(x,y,z) dV; evaluated via iterated integrals; computable in cylindrical (r,θ,z) and spherical (ρ,φ,θ) coordinates via the Jacobian.

concepts.name: Change of Variables (Jacobian)
concepts.description: For the substitution (x,y) = T(u,v): ∬ f dA = ∬ f(T(u,v))|∂(x,y)/∂(u,v)| du dv; the Jacobian determinant is the scaling factor.

concepts.name: Line Integrals
concepts.description: Integrals along a curve C: scalar line integral ∫_C f ds measures arc-length-weighted sum; vector line integral ∫_C F·dr computes work done by force field F.

concepts.name: Surface Integrals
concepts.description: Integrals over a surface S; scalar: ∬_S f dS; vector (flux): ∬_S F·dS = ∬_S F·n dS; key for Stokes' and Gauss' theorems.

concepts.name: Vector Fields
concepts.description: An assignment of a vector to each point in a region; a field is conservative if F = ∇f for some potential f, in which case ∫_C F·dr is path-independent.

concepts.name: Curl and Divergence
concepts.description: Curl ∇×F measures rotational tendency; divergence ∇·F measures outward flux density; both are differential operators on vector fields.

concepts.name: Green's Theorem
concepts.description: Relates a line integral around a simple closed curve C to a double integral over the region D it encloses; a special case of Stokes' theorem in 2D.

concepts.name: Stokes' Theorem
concepts.description: ∬_S (∇×F)·dS = ∮_C F·dr, where C is the boundary of surface S; generalizes Green's theorem to 3D; a special case of the generalized Stokes' theorem on manifolds.

concepts.name: Divergence Theorem
concepts.description: ∭_E ∇·F dV = ∬_S F·dS, where S is the boundary of solid E; relates the total divergence inside a region to the outward flux through its boundary.

concepts.name: Fourier Series (Introduction)
concepts.description: Representing a periodic function as a sum of sines and cosines: f(x) = a₀/2 + Σ(aₙcos(nx) + bₙsin(nx)); coefficients given by orthogonality integrals.

concepts.name: Limits of Sequences (Calculus)
concepts.description: The limit of a sequence as n → ∞; computed using limit laws, l'Hôpital's rule (via continuous function), and the squeeze theorem.
